channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Lawmakers reveal whether Americans should pick up the Medicaid tab for illegal immigrants
item.description: Fox News Digital asked lawmakers on Capitol Hill if American taxpayers should have to pick up the tab for Medicaid for illegal immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How a fight over proxy voting for new parents upended the US House
item.description: The U.S. House is rethinking its voting rules after a push from Republican Rep. Anna Paulina Luna to allow proxy voting for new parents.
item.dc:creator: Leah Askarinam, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Market volatility complicates work for lawmakers in Alaska, a state whose fortunes are tied to oil
item.description: President Donald Trump's on-again, off-again tariffs and a related drop in oil prices have contributed to the uncertainty for Alaska lawmakers tasked each year with crafting a state budget based on volatile oil prices.
item.dc:creator: Becky Bohrer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil can be deported, immigration judge rules
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — Columbia University graduate student Mahmoud Khalil can be forced out of the country as a national security risk, an immigration judge in Louisiana ruled Friday after lawyers argued the legality of deporting the activist who participated in pro-Palestinian demonstrations.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump reaches deals with 5 law firms, allowing them to avoid prospect of punishing executive orders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced deals Friday with five law firms that will allow them to avoid the prospect of punishing executive orders and require them to together provide hundreds of millions of dollars' worth of free legal services for causes his administration says it suppor
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration scores win as judge clears way for ICE enforcement in churches, places of worship
item.description: A federal judge has ruled that ICE agents can make arrests at churches and other places of worship, rejecting claims from religious groups of rights violations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California a ‘broken state': Governor hopeful Stephen Cloobeck
item.description: Cloobeck's ideas to turn things around include revamping California’s entertainment tax credit program.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Tariffs could impact Tampa Bay shoppers, businesses by summer, economist says
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — Cheri Hudson owns clothing store Imagine That!!! in South Tampa. It has been her business for 25 years. She is a supporter of President Trump’s trade policies. “I trust Donald Trump. It's only going to be a matter of time. The tariffs are fair.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dem states to blame for most of the nearly $400 million in unemployment fraud over last 4 years, DOGE says
item.description: DOGE found that three states, all dominated by Democratic Party control, accounted for most of the hundreds of millions of dollars in unemployment fraud seen over the last four years under former President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Picture of Trump after the assassination attempt displaces Obama portrait at the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House on Friday hung a painting of President Donald Trump depicting a moment after an assassination attempt against him last summer — bumping the official portrait of one of his predecessors, Barack Obama.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Deadline arrives as Nevada lawmakers work furiously to pass bills out of committee
item.description: A deadline today will be the death knell for some of the nearly 1,100 bills in the Nevada Legislature. Some of the bills never went to a vote. Others were dead on arrival, identified by Gov. Joe Lombardo as bills he would veto.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Now that they've passed a budget plan, the hard part begins for Republicans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans narrowly got their budget plan over the finish line. Now comes the hard part. The resolution adopted this week was only a first step that allows Republicans to draft legislation that they can push through Congress without Democratic support.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Beijing slaps 125% tariffs on U.S. goods in latest U.S.-China trade escalation
item.description: China signals the latest tariff hike will be its last round of tit-for-tat measures, prompting sharp falls in European shares, as Asian stocks end the day mixed.
item.dc:creator: Sherisse Pham, MPR News

item.title: Social Security lists thousands of living immigrants as dead to prompt them to leave, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has moved to classify more than 6,000 living immigrants as dead, canceling their Social Security numbers and effectively wiping out their ability to work or receive benefits in an effort to get them to leave the country, according to two people familiar wit
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tariff reversal may have enriched cronies: Schiff
item.description: One of President Trump’s top political rivals, Democratic Sen. Adam Schiff of California, is demanding an investigation.
item.dc:creator: Blake Burman, NewsNation

item.title: Judge allows requirement that everyone in the US illegally must register to move forward
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday allowed the Trump administration to move forward with a requirement that everyone in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas movie studio plan changing with new players, tech/education emphasis
item.description: A global player, MBS Group, is stepping into the plan for a $900 million movie studio at the UNLV Harry Reid Research and Technology Park, near the 215 Beltway and Durango Drive in the southwest Las Vegas valley. Birtcher Development LLC is the project's developer.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Stocks slide as Trump's trade war with China spooks Wall Street
item.description: Stocks slid Thursday as President Trump’s escalating trade war with China and concerns about its economic implications continued to rattle Wall Street.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump orders agencies to ‘sunset’ environmental protections
item.description: President Trump directed agencies that regulate energy and the environment to sunset a wide array of environmental protections in an executive order issued Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: American Airlines flight carrying US lawmakers struck at DCA
item.description: No injuries were reported and the plane was on the ground at the time of impact, according to the agency.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Trump reverses Biden rule restricting water usage for showerheads and other home appliances
item.description: President Donald Trump has long complained about modern rules that limit water flow for showerheads, making it harder for him to wash his "beautiful hair.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump orders probe into 'Anonymous' New York Times editorial writer
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday signed a pair of memorandums directing the Department of Justice to investigate two individuals who served in his first administration and pushed back against Trump.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump limits tariffs on most nations for 90 days, except China
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125%.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Banning books prompts emotional testimony on both sides of proposed Nevada law
item.description: Parents, political groups and a member of the Clark County school board slammed legislation that is aimed at ending local decisions to pull books off school library shelves during a legislative hearing in Carson City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: House GOP leaders prep vote on budget despite rift within the party
item.description: Republicans in Congress are closer to passing key elements of President Trump's legislative agenda — like extending tax cuts that expire at the end of the year — but only if the House and Senate can get on the same page.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration plans to strip NYC of $188M for shelter program: Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The Trump administration is planning to strip New York City of $188 million, Mayor Eric Adams announced on Tuesday. The $188 million includes more than $80 million that the Federal Emergency Management Agency clawed back from the city in February.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump planning military parade through DC for 79th birthday
item.description: The Trump administration is planning a June 14 military parade through the streets of Washington, D.C., to mark the president's 79th birthday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Trump threatens additional 50 percent tariff on China
item.description: The White House had already imposed a 20 percent tariff on Chinese imports over fentanyl concerns, meaning China could face an effective tariff rate as high as 70 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump expected to host Netanyahu at White House next week
item.description: President Trump is expected to host Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu for a visit on Monday, a White House official told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC
